questions.question: The Panchayats Extension to Scheduled Areas (PESA) Act, 1996, is related to which level of government in India?
questions.explanation: It is related to the empowerment of Gram Sabhas.

questions.question: Which of the following rights is not a fundamental right under the Indian Constitution?
options: Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression
questions.explanation: Right to Education is a fundamental duty.

questions.question: The 73rd Amendment to the Indian Constitution, 1992, is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: It provided for the establishment of three-tier Panchayat System.

questions.question: The Indian Constitution has borrowed the concept of 'Basic Structure' from?
questions.explanation: It has been borrowed from the United States Constitution.

questions.question: The West Bengal Panchayat Elections Act, 2003, is related to?
options: Reservation of Seats for Backward Classes
questions.explanation: It provided for the reservation of seats for women.

questions.question: Which of the following major rivers flows through the Sundarbans in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganges River flows through the Sundarbans.

questions.question: Which mountain range runs along the northwestern part of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Sikkim Himalayas run along the northwestern part of West Bengal.

questions.question: West Bengal's largest lake is located in which district?
questions.explanation: The largest lake in West Bengal is located in the Malda district.

questions.question: Which of the following cities is the administrative headquarters of the Purulia district?
questions.explanation: Purulia is the administrative headquarters of the Purulia district.

questions.question: West Bengal shares its international border with which country?
questions.explanation: West Bengal shares its international border with Bangladesh.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes was launched by the Government of West Bengal in 2025 to promote women's empowerment?
questions.explanation: Launched to provide financial assistance to unmarried women.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is the main source of water for the Gobindapur water treatment plant in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It supplies drinking water to Kolkata.

questions.question: Which of the following is a major initiative of the Government of West Bengal to promote the use of electric vehicles?
questions.explanation: Launched to reduce carbon emissions.

questions.question: Which of the following festivals was celebrated by the Government of West Bengal in a grand manner in 2026?
questions.explanation: Celebrated with grand fairs and cultural events.

questions.question: Which of the following is a major initiative of the Government of West Bengal to promote the use of solar energy?
questions.explanation: To reduce dependence on fossil fuels.

questions.question: Which of the following government schemes aims to provide financial assistance to the families of West Bengal's martyred personnel?
options: Swami Vivekananda Merit Cum Means Scholarship
questions.explanation: The Siksha Sahayata Scheme provides financial assistance to families of martyred personnel.

questions.question: The Swasthya Bhita Scheme was launched by the West Bengal government in the year?
questions.explanation: The Swasthya Bhita Scheme was launched in 2016.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of the Karmayogi Prakalpa scheme launched by the West Bengal government?
options: To provide skill development training to youth
To provide employment opportunities to the unemployed
To promote digital literacy among the rural population
questions.explanation: The Karmayogi Prakalpa scheme aims to provide skill development training to youth.

questions.question: The Unnayan Scheme was launched by the West Bengal government to?
options: Provide education to the children of migrant workers
Provide skill development training to the youth
Provide employment opportunities to the unemployed
Provide financial assistance to the families of martyred personnel
questions.explanation: The Unnayan Scheme aims to provide employment opportunities to the unemployed.

questions.question: Which of the following is a flagship scheme of the West Bengal government under the Jal Jeevan Mission?
questions.explanation: The Nal Jeevan Abhiyan is a flagship scheme of the West Bengal government under the Jal Jeevan Mission.

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an article at a 25% discount and still gets a profit of 20%. The cost price of the article is?
questions.explanation: Using the formula: Selling Price = Cost Price + Profit, and given a 25% discount on the selling price.

questions.question: A person invested Rs. 8,000 in a business. If the profit is Rs. 2,400, then his share of profit will be?
questions.explanation: Calculating the ratio of profit share based on the investment made.

questions.question: A man buys an article for Rs. 900 and sells it at a loss of 10%. The selling price of the article is?
questions.explanation: Calculating the selling price after a 10% loss from the cost price.

questions.question: A person buys a toy for Rs. 750 and sells it for Rs. 900. His gain percentage is?
questions.explanation: Calculating the percentage gain based on the cost price and selling price.

questions.question: A businessman buys wheat at Rs. 15 per kg and sells it at Rs. 18 per kg. His profit percentage is?
questions.explanation: Calculating the profit percentage based on the cost price and selling price per kg.

questions.question: A person invested Rs. 10,000 at 10% compound interest for 2 years. What will be the amount after 2 years?
questions.explanation: Interest is compounded annually.

questions.question: Anuradha borrowed Rs. 80,000 from a bank at 15% simple interest per annum. How much interest will be paid after 3 years?
questions.explanation: Simple interest is calculated using the formula SI = P * R * T / 100.

questions.question: If the simple interest on a sum of money at 12% per annum for 2 years is Rs. 1,536, what is the principal amount?
questions.explanation: Principal amount can be calculated using the formula P = SI * 100 / R * T.

questions.question: What is the rate of interest at which a sum of money will become 1.5 times the principal amount in 5 years?
questions.explanation: Using the compound interest formula: A = P * (1 + R/100)^T.

questions.question: Rashmi deposited Rs. 5,000 in a savings account that earns 8% compound interest per annum. How much will she have in her account after 3 years?
questions.explanation: Compound interest is calculated using the formula A = P * (1 + R/100)^T.

questions.question: A pipe can fill 1/3rd of a tank in 1 hour. If the tank can hold 2400 litres of water, in how much time will the pipe fill 75% of the tank?
questions.explanation: Time taken to fill 75% of the tank is calculated by finding the time taken to fill 75/100 of the tank.

questions.question: Three persons A, B and C can together complete a work in 4 days. In how many days will A and B together complete the work?
questions.explanation: The combined work rate of A and B is the same as their combined work rate with C.

questions.question: A man can do a piece of work in 15 days. He works at it for 5 days and then his son helps him and together they complete the remaining work in 2 days. How long will the son take to do the whole work?
questions.explanation: The son's work rate can be found by comparing his work rate with his father's.

questions.question: A group of friends can complete a task in 24 hours, working 6 hours a day. If 1/4th of the group members are absent, then how many hours will it take to complete the task?
questions.explanation: The work rate decreases when the number of workers decreases.

questions.question: A pump can fill a tank in 2 hours. However, due to a leak, it will take 8 hours to fill the tank. If the tank is already 1/4th filled, how long will it take to fill the remaining 3/4th of the tank?
questions.explanation: The pump's work rate is reduced by the leak, affecting the time taken to fill the tank.

questions.question: A train covers a distance of 120 km in 2 hours. How much distance will it cover in 4 hours?
questions.explanation: Speed is constant, so distance will be directly proportional to time.

questions.question: A car travels at an average speed of 60 km/h for 2 hours. What is the distance covered?
questions.explanation: Distance is calculated by multiplying speed and time.

questions.question: A bus travels from Kolkata to Digha at an average speed of 80 km/h. If the distance between the two places is 240 km, how much time will it take?
questions.explanation: Time is calculated by dividing distance by speed.

questions.question: A person travels from Kolkata to Howrah at an average speed of 25 km/h. If the distance between the two places is 30 km, how much time will it take?
questions.explanation: Time is calculated by dividing distance by speed.

questions.question: A train travels from Sealdah to Barrackpore at an average speed of 40 km/h. If the distance between the two places is 120 km, what is the time taken?
questions.explanation: Time is calculated by dividing distance by speed.

questions.question: Sudeshna is the daughter of Rohan's wife's mother. How is Sudeshna related to Rohan?
questions.explanation: Sudeshna is Rohan's sister-in-law.

questions.question: Arjun is the son of a man whose son is the father of a girl. How is Arjun related to this girl?
questions.explanation: Arjun is the girl's father.

questions.question: A person's mother's father's father is his _______.
questions.explanation: A person's mother's father's father is his maternal grandfather's father.

questions.question: Sneha is the daughter of a man whose only child is a son. Which of the following is a blood relation of Sneha?
questions.explanation: Sneha's father's wife is her mother.

questions.question: Bimal's daughter is married to Chiranjit's son. Who is the maternal grandmother of Chiranjit?
questions.explanation: The maternal grandmother of Chiranjit is Chiranjit's wife's mother.

questions.question: In the series 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, 16, find the next term.
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding consecutive increments.

questions.question: Find the next letter in the series A, D, G, J, M, ?.
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 3 letters in the alphabet.

questions.question: In the series 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, find the next term.
questions.explanation: The pattern is increasing by adding 4, 6, 8, 10, etc.

questions.question: Find the next letter in the series C, F, I, L, O, ?.
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 3 letters in the alphabet.

questions.question: In the series 1, 3, 6, 10, 15, find the next term.
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding consecutive increments.
